questions.question_text:
In this task, you will value a set of 100 boxes, each containing some dollar amount.

The payoff rule: {{ payoff_rule_description }}.

In this set, {{ n_high_boxes }} boxes contain ${{ high_payoff_usd }} and {{ n_low_boxes }} boxes contain ${{ low_payoff_usd }}.

{{ payoff_rule_example }}

Option A: {{ option_a_label }}
Option B: Receive a certain dollar amount

You start with an endowment of ${{ endowment_usd }}. Any gains or losses are added to or subtracted from this endowment.

What is the certain dollar amount (Option B) at which you would be exactly indifferent between Option A and Option B? Please state a dollar value between ${{ min_value_usd }} and ${{ max_value_usd }}.

In this task you will choose between:
- Option A: A sure payment of $0
- Option B: {{ option_b_loss_aversion_description }}

{{ payoff_rule_description_la }}

The loss amount is fixed at -${{ fixed_loss_abs_usd }}. The gain amount $X increases in ${{ gain_step_usd }} steps.

You start with an endowment of ${{ endowment_usd }}.

What is the minimum value of $X at which you would prefer Option B over Option A (i.e., prefer the {{ task_type }} over the sure $0)? Please state a dollar value (must be a positive number).
